changes.description: New file — 20-line guide: what this is, how to run locally, how to read results
Shared declare-const context for all extracted snippets: client, channel, message, post, comment, community, user, poll, file types plus string IDs and UI helper stubs
24 cohort-balanced pages: chat-track (Eastern hot-path) + social-track + shared. Includes 4 enumerate: entries for directories resolved at extract time
strict+noEmit tsconfig pointing at AmityTypescriptSDK/packages/sdk/src/index.ts via paths. Includes preamble.d.ts and results/extracted/**/*.ts

Walks pages.json, resolves enumerate: entries, extracts TS/JS/typescript/ts fenced blocks (handles indented MDX variants), writes one .ts per block to results/extracted/. Also writes _manifest.json for the runner.
Runs tsc --noEmit --project across all extracted blocks. Parses per-file errors, emits results/latest.json with stats + failure details grouped by source page
First-run report: 28 pages, 76 blocks extracted, 64 passed (84.2%), 12 failed

failure_breakdown.syntax_fragments_5: flag-unflag-a-message.mdx blocks 3-6 contain class method bodies extracted out of their class context (TS1434: Unexpected keyword or identifier). These are partial code examples not meant to be standalone. Fix in v2 by detecting and wrapping bare method bodies in a class shell, or by annotating those blocks with // @ts-nocheck.
failure_breakdown.jsx_errors_4: file.mdx block 1, image-handling.mdx block 1, video-handling.mdx block 1, viewing-content.mdx block 7 — all contain JSX (React components with <input type=...> etc). The tsconfig does not have jsx enabled. Fix in v2: add 'jsx': 'react-jsx' and 'lib': ['dom', 'es2020'] to tsconfig. These are not real doc errors.
failure_breakdown.real_doc_errors_3: authentication.mdx blocks 4 & 8: object literal missing trailing comma after displayName before authToken. get-user-information.mdx blocks 1 & 2 also fail but are also JSX components (React hooks + JSX return) — these need jsx support too. The auth comma is a real doc bug worth fixing.

notes.notable_finding: Zero preamble gaps on first run — all context variables declared correctly. High 84.2% pass rate vs expected 40-60% confirms preamble quality. The 12 failures break down as: 5 extraction-scope limitation, 4 JSX config needed, 3 real issues (2 auth comma bugs + 1 mixed JSX). Only ~3 failures represent genuine doc accuracy problems.
follow_up_tasks_recommended: Add jsx:react-jsx + dom lib to tsconfig to handle React snippet blocks
Detect and wrap bare class-method fragments in a class body shell during extraction
Fix auth.mdx blocks 4 & 8: trailing comma missing in login() object literal
Wire into check-drift.py CI gate once false-positive rate below 10% (currently 9 of 12 failures are false positives, ~11.8% false-positive rate)
